codeReview.summary: The refactor cleanly extracts shared connection helpers and leaves `createProfile` / `resolveProfile` behavior unchanged, but it does not fully satisfy the feature's own expected behavior and it still lacks the unit-test evidence required for VAL-TYPES-004.

issues.description: The feature spec says `ProfileConfig` and `ProviderInstance` should share the extracted base type, but the implementation only makes `ProviderInstanceConnection` extend `Partial<ProviderConnectionConfig>` and then keeps a separate `providerType` field on `ProviderInstance` at lines 41-47. Because the base `provider` field is still translated manually in `extractConnectionFromProvider`, generic code still cannot treat `ProfileConfig` and `ProviderInstance` as the same shared shape, so this expected behavior is only partially implemented.

VAL-TYPES-004 requires unit-test evidence that `ProfileConfig`, `ProviderInstance`, `createProfile`, and `resolveProfile` remain backward compatible, but the unit-test runner still registers no profile/provider compatibility suite and the feature handoff reports `tests.added: []`. I found no repo test coverage for `createProfile` or `resolveProfile`, so the required regression evidence for this feature is still missing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill does not match how this repo's unit runner works closely enough for contract-driven refactors: it recommends `npm run test:unit -- --grep '<relevant-pattern>'` and says to add tests first when none exist, but this worker used the grep pattern anyway and still shipped the feature with no dedicated VAL-TYPES-004 tests. The skill should mention that the unit runner is hard-coded and should explicitly require adding a focused suite when a validation contract depends on unit-test evidence.

The shared architecture library is stale after this refactor. It still documents `packages/shared/src/providers.ts` as the provider-types module, but the repo now uses `connection-config.ts` and `provider-instance.ts`. Future workers reviewing shared-package structure will be sent to outdated file names.

summary: Review failed. The refactor preserves the existing helper behavior, but it only partially implements the requested shared base-type relationship for provider/profile types and still lacks the dedicated unit-test evidence required by VAL-TYPES-004. I also noted shared-state gaps in the refactor-worker skill guidance and the stale architecture library entry for shared provider modules.
